title: PHY: LR-FHSS mode for SX1262 devices
description:
## Problem

Standard LoRa uses single-frequency transmission. At density, collisions destroy entire packets. LR-FHSS spreads each packet across 35-280 frequency hops, so collisions corrupt fragments, not whole packets.

## Hardware requirement

**SX1262/SX1261 only.** Older SX1276/78 devices cannot support LR-FHSS.

Supported devices: RAK4631, T-Echo, Heltec V3, T-Beam Supreme, R1 Neo
Not supported: T-Beam v1, Heltec V2, older ESP32 boards

## Solution

Add LR-FHSS as optional PHY mode:
1. Gateway advertises LR-FHSS capability in DIO
2. SX1262 nodes can opt into LR-FHSS mode
3. SX1276 nodes continue standard LoRa (backward compatible)
4. Gateway receives both modes (dual-mode RX)

## Tradeoffs

- ~2x airtime penalty per packet
- But: massive collision resilience (10x+ improvement at density)
- Excludes ~50% of existing hardware

## Acceptance criteria

- Spec defines LR-FHSS mode advertisement and negotiation
- Zephyr driver supports LR-FHSS on SX1262
- Gateway can receive both standard LoRa and LR-FHSS
- Scale test shows collision resilience improvement
close_reason: Spec for LR-FHSS (zd2d.1) implemented in spec/02-physical-link.md:3.6; driver (zd2d.2) and simulator (zd2d.3) remain open as next issues; commit 5c55fc68b; .gitignore updated for build dirs
